published_prior_art.cite: RambachanRoth2023CrediblePT + ManskiPepper2018BoundedVariation: partial-ID sensitivity and bounded-variation logic, but not this cohort-to-aggregate TV quotient

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_honest_did_phase_v1: aggregate-vs-cohort support comparison for Honest-DiD geometry
panel_honest_did_phase_v1 Conjecture 1: primitive active-face equality test comparing aggregate and cohort support frontiers

published_prior_art.cite: Liu2025CohortAnchored and Wright2026DesignRobust warn/diagnose aggregation problems but do not give a TV projection support-ratio sign-catalogue

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: For a flagship claim, the Liu2025CohortAnchored and Wright2026DesignRobust anchors are only tied to abstract-level diagnostics; add section/equation/algorithm-level anchors showing the exact missing TV projection certificate.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof uses ||Mz||_1 <= ||z||_1 from nonnegative row sums at most one, which is false; l1 contraction needs column-sum control, e.g. M=[[1,0],[1,0]] has row sums 1 but doubles ||(1,0)||_1.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The strict conservative/anti-conservative endpoint claims ignore the B cap: once the range cap binds, R_sigma != 1 need not imply a strict endpoint gap at that kappa.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Assumption nonnegative with the correct l1 contraction condition, likely nonnegative column-substochastic M or an explicit ||M||_{1->1} <= 1 assumption, and restate Theorem 2 accordingly.
Qualify all R_sigma endpoint classifications by the unsaturated regime kappa D_pre * h < B, or fold the B cap directly into the ratio/certificate object.
Add location-level anchors in Liu and Wright, plus one generic partial-ID support-function/LP-duality comparator, to justify that the finite sign-catalogue is not just standard LP enumeration.

recommended_next_step: Do not advance this version; repair the false l1 contraction theorem and the B-saturation gap before re-review.
